name: USS Aegir (AS 23)
events: 21 Dec 1944 At 0920 Y/21, USS Searaven (T/Lt.Cdr. R. Berthrong, USN) arrived at Midway from her patrol area in the northern part of the South China Sea. She was then taken in hand for some minor voyage repairs and fuelling by USS Aegir (T/Capt. A.L. Prosser, USN).
At 1430 Y/21, USS Searaven departed Midway for Pearl Harbor. (2)

29 Dec 1944 At 1000 Y/29, USS Tilefish (T/Cdr. R.M. Keithly, USN) arrived at Midway. She departed at 1500 Y/29 for Pearl Harbor. In the meantime some voyage repairs were made by USS Aegir (T/Capt. A.L. Prosser, USN). (3)

26 Apr 1945 Around 0915 Y/26, USS Hackleback (T/Lt.Cdr. F.E. Janney, USN) ended her 1st war patrol at Midway. She was then taken in hand for a tender refit by USS Aegir (T/Capt. J.M. Boyd, USN). The refit was completed on 11 May 1945. She conducted five days of exercises off Midway before being ready to sail on her 2nd patrol.

22 Aug 1945 USS Devilfish (T/Lt.Cdr. S.S. Mann, Jr., USN) ended her 4th war patrol at Midway. She had met the escort around 0600 Y/22. At Midway she was taken in hand for some repairs by the submarine tender USS Aegir (T/Capt. J.M. Boyd, USN).

1 Sep 1945 The submarine tender USS Aegir (T/Capt. J.M. Boyd, USN) and the submarines USS Gurnard (T/Lt.Cdr. G.S. Simmons, 3rd, USN), USS Devilfish (T/Lt.Cdr. S.S. Mann, Jr., USN), USS Hackleback (T/Cdr. F.E. Janney, USN), USS Lionfish (T/Lt.Cdr. B.M. Ganyard, USN), USS Moray (T/Cdr. F.L. Barrows, USN), USS Aspro (T/Cdr. J.H. Ashley, Jr., USN) and USS Piranha (T/Cdr. D.G. Irvine, USN) departed Midway for the Mare Island Navy Yard where they arrived on 11 September.
